result.last_assistant_message:
Fixed the inventory reconciler. The implementation now:
- Parses and applies movement rules (shipment/return/adjustment add, sale/damage subtract)
- Calculates expected stock correctly (on_hand + movement_delta)
- Filters out zero-delta items and sorts by absolute delta descending, then SKU ascending
- Returns proper "short" status for negative deltas
- Formats reports with all required fields (sku, expected, counted, delta, status)

All tests pass: `mix test` shows 0 failures.

recovered.description: Seeded implementation ignores movement rules and fails until repaired.

steps.summary:
file	README.md
dir	_build
dir	lib
file	mix.exs
dir	test

# Inventory Audit

`InventoryAudit.anomalies/3` receives three CSV-like strings:

- stock: `sku,on_hand`
- movements: `type,sku,quantity`
- counts: `sku,counted`

Movement rules:

- `shipment`, `return`, and `adjustment` add quantity.
- `sale` and `damage` subtract quantity.

Expected stock is starting on_hand plus all movements for a SKU.
Delta is counted minus expected. Only non-zero deltas are anomalies.
A positive delta has status `over`; a negative delta has status `short`.
Sort anomalies by absolute delta descending, then by SKU ascending.

`InventoryAudit.report/3` renders one line per anomaly:

`sku=<sku> expected=<expected> counted=<counted> delta=<delta> status=<status>`

defmodule InventoryAudit do
  @moduledoc false

  def anomalies(stock_csv, _movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock = parse_pairs(stock_csv)
    counts = parse_pairs(count_csv)

    stock
    |> Enum.map(fn {sku, expected} ->
      counted = Map.get(counts, sku, expected)
      delta = counted - expected

      %{
        sku: sku,
        expected: expected,
        counted: counted,
        delta: delta,
        status: if(delta > 0, do: "over", else: "ok")
      }
    end)
  end

  def report(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock_csv
    |> anomalies(movement_csv, count_csv)
    |> Enum.map_join("\n", fn anomaly ->
      "sku=#{anomaly.sku} expected=#{anomaly.expected} counted=#{anomaly.counted}"
    end)
  end

  defp parse_pairs(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [sku, value] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      {sku, String.to_integer(value)}
    end)
  end
end

args.new_string:
defmodule InventoryAudit do
  @moduledoc false

  def anomalies(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock = parse_pairs(stock_csv)
    movements = parse_movements(movement_csv)
    counts = parse_pairs(count_csv)

    # Get all unique SKUs from stock and counts
    all_skus = Map.keys(stock) ++ Map.keys(counts) |> Enum.uniq()

    all_skus
    |> Enum.map(fn sku ->
      on_hand = Map.get(stock, sku, 0)
      movement_delta = Map.get(movements, sku, 0)
      expected = on_hand + movement_delta
      counted = Map.get(counts, sku, expected)
      delta = counted - expected

      %{
        sku: sku,
        expected: expected,
        counted: counted,
        delta: delta,
        status: status(delta)
      }
    end)
    |> Enum.filter(fn %{delta: delta} -> delta != 0 end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn %{sku: sku, delta: delta} ->
      {abs(delta) * -1, sku}
    end)
  end

  def report(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock_csv
    |> anomalies(movement_csv, count_csv)
    |> Enum.map_join("\n", fn anomaly ->
      "sku=#{anomaly.sku} expected=#{anomaly.expected} counted=#{anomaly.counted} delta=#{anomaly.delta} status=#{anomaly.status}"
    end)
  end

  defp parse_pairs(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [sku, value] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      {sku, String.to_integer(value)}
    end)
  end

  defp parse_movements(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Enum.reduce(%{}, fn line, acc ->
      [type, sku, qty_str] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      quantity = String.to_integer(qty_str)
      delta = movement_delta(type, quantity)
      Map.update(acc, sku, delta, &(&1 + delta))
    end)
  end

  defp movement_delta("shipment", qty), do: qty
  defp movement_delta("return", qty), do: qty
  defp movement_delta("adjustment", qty), do: qty
  defp movement_delta("sale", qty), do: -qty
  defp movement_delta("damage", qty), ...

defmodule InventoryAudit do
  @moduledoc false

  def anomalies(stock_csv, _movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock = parse_pairs(stock_csv)
    counts = parse_pairs(count_csv)

    stock
    |> Enum.map(fn {sku, expected} ->
      counted = Map.get(counts, sku, expected)
      delta = counted - expected

      %{
        sku: sku,
        expected: expected,
        counted: counted,
        delta: delta,
        status: if(delta > 0, do: "over", else: "ok")
      }
    end)
  end

  def report(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock_csv
    |> anomalies(movement_csv, count_csv)
    |> Enum.map_join("\n", fn anomaly ->
      "sku=#{anomaly.sku} expected=#{anomaly.expected} counted=#{anomaly.counted}"
    end)
  end

  defp parse_pairs(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [sku, value] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      {sku, String.to_integer(value)}
    end)
  end
end

defmodule InventoryAudit do
  @moduledoc false

  def anomalies(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock = parse_pairs(stock_csv)
    movements = parse_movements(movement_csv)
    counts = parse_pairs(count_csv)

    # Get all unique SKUs from stock and counts
    all_skus = Map.keys(stock) ++ Map.keys(counts) |> Enum.uniq()

    all_skus
    |> Enum.map(fn sku ->
      on_hand = Map.get(stock, sku, 0)
      movement_delta = Map.get(movements, sku, 0)
      expected = on_hand + movement_delta
      counted = Map.get(counts, sku, expected)
      delta = counted - expected

      %{
        sku: sku,
        expected: expected,
        counted: counted,
        delta: delta,
        status: status(delta)
      }
    end)
    |> Enum.filter(fn %{delta: delta} -> delta != 0 end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn %{sku: sku, delta: delta} ->
      {abs(delta) * -1, sku}
    end)
  end

  def report(stock_csv, movement_csv, count_csv) do
    stock_csv
    |> anomalies(movement_csv, count_csv)
    |> Enum.map_join("\n", fn anomaly ->
      "sku=#{anomaly.sku} expected=#{anomaly.expected} counted=#{anomaly.counted} delta=#{anomaly.delta} status=#{anomaly.status}"
    end)
  end

  defp parse_pairs(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [sku, value] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      {sku, String.to_integer(value)}
    end)
  end

  defp parse_movements(csv) do
    csv
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.drop(1)
    |> Enum.reduce(%{}, fn line, acc ->
      [type, sku, qty_str] = String.split(line, ",", trim: true)
      quantity = String.to_integer(qty_str)
      delta = movement_delta(type, quantity)
      Map.update(acc, sku, delta, &(&1 + delta))
    end)
  end

  defp movement_delta("shipment", qty), do: qty
  defp movement_delta("return", qty), do: qty
  defp movement_delta("adjustment", qty), do: qty
  defp movement_delta("sale", qty)...

args.command: cd /workspace && mix test 2>&1 | grep -q '0 failures' && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

args.report:
Fixed lib/inventory_audit.ex:

1. Added parse_movements/1 to parse movement CSV and calculate net delta per SKU
2. Added movement_delta/2 helper to apply movement rules (add for shipment/return/adjustment, subtract for sale/damage)
3. Modified anomalies/3 to:
   - Combine stock and count SKUs for complete coverage
   - Apply movements to calculate expected stock
   - Filter out zero-delta items
   - Sort by absolute delta descending, then SKU ascending
   - Fix status logic to return "short" for negative deltas
4. Fixed report/3 to include delta and status fields in output

Verification: mix test passes with 0 failures.
args.summary: Repaired InventoryAudit module to correctly reconcile inventory. The implementation now parses and applies movement rules (shipment/return/adjustment add, sale/damage subtract), calculates expected stock, filters out zero-delta items, sorts anomalies by absolute delta descending then SKU ascending, and properly formats the report with all fields including delta and status.
